zh.Bewsey, Warrington, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾貝福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane le Boteler於1205年12月20日出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾貝福時，她的父親Thomas Pincerna20歲，母親Cecilia Arraby20歲。她於1247年與Sir Robert de Bulkeley在英國英格蘭爾布爾可勒結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於從1243年到1293年在英國爾布爾可勒。

zh-hans.Bewsey, Warrington, Lancashire, England: 英国英格兰藍卡爾貝福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Jane le Boteler于1205年12月20日出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾貝福时，她的父亲Thomas Pincerna20岁，母亲Cecilia Arraby20岁。她于1247年与Sir Robert de Bulkeley在英国英格兰爾布爾可勒结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于从1243年到1293年在英国爾布爾可勒。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane le Boteler was born on 20 December 1205, in Bewsey, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Pincerna, was 20 and her mother, Cecilia Arraby, was 20. She married Sir Robert de Bulkeley in 1247, in Bulkeley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died from 1243 to 1293, in Bulkeley, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Jane le Boteler est née le 20 décembre 1205, à Bewsey, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Pincerna, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Arraby, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert de Bulkeley en 1247, à Bulkeley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée du 1243 au 1293, à Bulkeley, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Jane le Boteler am 20. Dezember 1205 in Bewsey, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Pincerna 20 und ihre Mutter Cecilia Arraby 20. Sie hat 1247 in Bulkeley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Robert de Bulkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb von 1243 bis 1293 in Bulkeley, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane le Boteler è nata il il 20 dicembre 1205 nel Bewsey, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Pincerna, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Cecilia Arraby, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert de Bulkeley nel 1247, nel Bulkeley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì da 1243 a 1293, nel Bulkeley, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Jane le Botelerが1205年12月20日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアベウセイで生まれた時，父Thomas Pincernaは20歳，母Cecilia Arrabyは20歳であった。1247年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアブルケリーでSir Robert de Bulkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1243年まで1293年にイギリスチェシュアイアブルケリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane le Boteler(이)가 1205년 12월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 뷰시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Pincerna(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecilia Arraby(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1247년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버켈리에서 Sir Robert de Bulkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1243년 부터 1293년 까지 영국 체실르 버켈리에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane le Boteler nasceu em 20 de dezembro de 1205, em Bewsey, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Pincerna, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia Arraby, tinha 20 anos. Ela faleceu de 1243 a 1293, em Bulkeley, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Jane le Boteler родилась 20 декабря 1205 г. в местности бюси, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Pincerna было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Arraby, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert de Bulkeley в 1247 г. в местности булкели, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла с 1243 по 1293 в местности булкели, чешир, Англия.
